violations.description: Spec file size (225 lines) increases cognitive load and makes failures harder to localize quickly.
violations.suggestion: Split by acceptance criterion into smaller focused files with shared helper imports.

violations.description: Spec file size (180 lines) and broad scope reduce readability and maintenance speed.
violations.suggestion: Partition into policy-graph, report-summary, and local-diagnostics focused specs.

violations.description: Workflow parsing helpers are duplicated across multiple specs, increasing drift risk.
violations.suggestion: Extract escapeRegex/getJobBlock/getNeeds into a shared helper under tests/support/utils.

violations.description: Repeated helper implementation duplicates parsing semantics and adds maintenance overhead.
violations.suggestion: Import shared parser helpers to keep behavior consistent.

violations.description: Large boolean conjunctions inside single expects obscure which precondition failed.
violations.suggestion: Split assertions into named expect statements for faster failure diagnosis.

violations.description: Legacy [P0]/[P1] IDs reduce traceability consistency with story-scoped structured IDs used elsewhere.
violations.suggestion: Adopt structured IDs (e.g., E6-ATDD-E2E-XXX) for this suite.
violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] defines required policy-first quality-stage jobs ...')

recommendations: Split large spec files by acceptance criterion or behavior axis.
Extract duplicated workflow-parsing helpers into shared utilities.
Replace conjunction-style asserts with granular, named expects.
Standardize test ID schema across all CI policy specs.
summary: Maintainability is the main weakness due to long files, duplicated parsing helpers, and conjunction-heavy assertions.
